text.title: The twelve phases
text.summary: The orchestrator runs a fixed sequence of phases, each fronted by exactly one prompt file. Phases may be skipped if a domain playbook says so, but their order is invariant.

list.item: 1. INTAKE — analyzers fetch each ticket and produce a structured report with open questions.
2. CLARIFY — open questions are deduplicated and asked of the user in batched calls.
3. PLAN — one planning subagent per ticket writes the SDD artifact set (spec, design, test plan, implementation plan, atomic tasks).
4. PARALLELIZE — the orchestrator inspects file overlap and groups tickets into execution waves.
5. BASELINE — for visual fixes, capture a focused before-screenshot of the bug area with a red CSS outline.
6. EXECUTE — wave-scoped subagents apply the fix in isolated worktrees and merge into the local default branch (no remote push).
7. RESOLVE — when waves conflict, a dedicated resolver subagent reads both sides' intent and combines them.
8. VERIFY — build, type-check, lint, unit and end-to-end tests run against the merged result.
9. REVIEW — an independent reviewer reads each feature's diff against its spec and returns PASS / FAIL-WITH-NITS / FAIL.
10. FIX — review blockers are addressed by a fix-applier subagent, then VERIFY and REVIEW are repeated (hard cap on iterations).
11. RECAPTURE — focused after-screenshot with a green outline; before+after pair delivered out-of-band with explanatory captions.
12. DELIVER — tracker statuses updated, commits pushed and artifacts deployed only after explicit user confirmation.

section.title: Prompt file map
section.p: Each phase has exactly one prompt file. Naming uses a numeric prefix so the directory listing reads as the phase order.

list.item: `prompts/01-task-analyzer.md` — INTAKE
`prompts/02-clarification-collector.md` — CLARIFY
`prompts/03-sdd-planner.md` — PLAN
`prompts/04-baseline-capturer.md` — BASELINE
`prompts/05-task-executor.md` — EXECUTE
`prompts/06-merge-resolver.md` — RESOLVE
`prompts/07-build-verifier.md` — VERIFY (build + lint + tests)
`prompts/08-e2e-tester.md` — VERIFY (end-to-end)
`prompts/09-code-reviewer.md` — REVIEW
`prompts/10-fix-applier.md` — FIX
`prompts/11-recapture-and-deliver.md` — RECAPTURE
`prompts/12-deployer.md` — DELIVER

section.title: Skipping phases
section.p: A domain playbook may declare phases that do not apply. For example, a non-visual fix skips BASELINE and the after-capture step in RECAPTURE; a research-only batch may stop after PLAN. The orchestrator still walks the phase list in order — it just dispatches no subagent for skipped phases and records the skip in its run log.
